name: Synthetic Responses Are Acceptable as Pre-pilot Checks, Not Replacement Validation
statement: Frohn et al. frame labeled synthetic responses and challenge sets as acceptable pre-pilot diagnostic material for LLM constructed-response scoring, while keeping real student responses necessary for pilot validation.
population_or_context: Commercial LLM scoring of constructed-response assessment items.
time_horizon: Conference paper full text retrieved on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: The framework discusses agreement thresholds and metrics rather than reporting one empirical synthetic-response validation result.
caveats: Framework paper rather than one empirical dataset.
Synthetic responses are not positioned as sufficient deployment-readiness evidence.
Specific implementation thresholds depend on item, rubric, and scoring context.
evaluation_method: Agreement and error analysis against human labels during pre-pilot checks, followed by pilot validation on real student responses.
reported_metrics: Cohen kappa, quadratic weighted kappa, accuracy, F1, Matthews correlation coefficient, false-positive and false-negative rates, score distributions, and conditional accuracy.
limitations: Does not establish that synthetic responses can replace authentic pilot data.
Bounded to constructed-response LLM scoring workflows.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the pre-pilot validation and challenge-set workflow, including later real-response pilot testing.
